lines: Wilmot leads the pack on 11pts after Morocco did Scotland dirty with a 1-0 last night — he, Callum, Jim, and Butters all cashing in on that one, which is either brilliant teamwork or just smug coincidence.

Jim's day was a tale of two halves: Morocco chipping in on one end, then watching the Netherlands dismantle Sweden 5-1 on the other — Al absolutely loving that scoreline while Jim quietly pretends he never backed the Swedes.

The Foreman is in genuine crisis territory — Türkiye already rock bottom of Group D with zero points after losing to Paraguay, and now Curaçao face Ecuador today in what could generously be described as a must-not-lose-by-eight situation.
Ellis, meanwhile, has Paraguay picking up a surprise 3pts and Argentina purring in Group J — quietly dangerous, the sort of sweepstake run that makes you say nothing and just smile at your phone.

Today's knife to the throat: Tunisia vs Japan in Group F — Callum and Dene are both still pointless with Tunisia and absolutely need a result, while Dene's Bosnia sit on 1pt in Group B suggesting this could be a very long tournament for him.
